name: join
description: Groups adjacent children into a seamless joined element by removing interior borders and radii

meta.name: Join
meta.description: Groups adjacent children into a seamless joined element by removing interior borders and radii

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

examples.title: Search group
examples.description: Input and button joined into a single search control

examples.title: Segmented buttons
examples.description: Segmented button row with no gaps between items

whenToUse: Input + button search groups where the two controls should appear as one unit
Segmented button rows where buttons share borders
whenNotToUse: Independent adjacent buttons that should remain visually separate
SegmentedControl — it has its own built-in grouping and selection semantics

related.name: SegmentedControl
related.reason: SegmentedControl has built-in selection state; Join is purely a layout wrapper

related.name: InputGroup
related.reason: InputGroup handles input + addon joining; Join is the general-purpose grouping primitive

intent.a11yRationale: Join is a layout-only container (role="none"). Accessibility semantics belong on the individual child controls — buttons carry their own role, inputs their labels.

flexibility.note: Horizontal and vertical grouping are both supported via the orientation prop
